title: Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation
Identify key diagnostic features of Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation
Apply clinical reasoning to Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation management
Recognize common pitfalls in Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation

sections.title: Overview of Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation
sections.content:
**Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation**

Endochondral vs intramembranous ossification, achondroplasia (FGFR3), osteogenesis imperfecta (collagen I), thanatophoric dysplasia

**Key Points:**
• Achondroplasia: FGFR3 gain-of-function mutation → impaired endochondral ossification → rhizomelic (proximal) limb shortening
• Osteogenesis imperfecta: Type I collagen defect → brittle bones, blue sclerae, hearing loss
• Thanatophoric dysplasia: lethal, FGFR3 mutation, cloverleaf skull

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Achondroplasia: FGFR3 gain-of-function mutation → impaired endochondral ossification → rhizomelic (proximal) limb shortening
• Osteogenesis imperfecta: Type I collagen defect → brittle bones, blue sclerae, hearing loss

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Thanatophoric dysplasia: lethal, FGFR3 mutation, cloverleaf skull
• Endochondral: cartilage → bone (long bones, vertebrae, pelvis)
• Intramembranous: mesenchyme → bone directly (skull flat bones, clavicle)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Skeletal Dysplasia & Bone Formation:**

• Thanatophoric dysplasia: lethal, FGFR3 mutation, cloverleaf skull
• Endochondral: cartilage → bone (long bones, vertebrae, pelvis)
• Intramembranous: mesenchyme → bone directly (skull flat bones, clavicle)

options.text: Achondroplasia: FGFR3 gain-of-function mutation → impaired endochondral ossification → rhizomelic (proximal) limb shortening
